question.text: Which of the following statements about commodity futures liquidity is correct?

answer.text: Futures contracts are highly liquid and can be closed out easily before expiry
Futures contracts can only be closed at maturity
Forward contracts are more liquid than futures
Futures contracts cannot be transferred to other parties

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Futures are highly liquid because they are standardized and exchange-traded — positions can be closed out easily by taking an opposite position before expiry. **The Trap:** Futures can be closed any time (not just maturity). Forwards are LESS liquid. Futures positions are transferable through offsetting trades on the exchange. **Cross-Exam Context:** Liquidity advantage of exchange-traded vs OTC is covered in all derivative exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Liquidity concentrates in near-month contracts — clients should avoid far-month contracts unless they specifically need longer-dated hedging, as spreads may be wider.
